server.c(3) Library Functions Manual server.c(3)

NAME

server.c

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t acds_server_init (acds_server_t *server, const acds_config_t *config)
Initialize discovery server. asciichat_error_t acds_server_run (acds_server_t *server)
Run discovery server main loop. void acds_server_shutdown (acds_server_t *server)
Shutdown discovery server. void * acds_client_handler (void *arg)
Per-client connection handler (thread entry point). void * acds_websocket_client_handler (void *arg)
WebSocket client handler (thread entry point).

Function Documentation

void * acds_client_handler (void * arg)

Per-client connection handler (thread entry point). Processes ACIP packets from a connected client. Handles crypto handshake, then dispatches packets to session/signaling handlers.

Parameters

arg Pointer to client connection context

Returns

NULL (thread exit value)

Definition at line 859 of file src/discovery-service/server.c.

859                                      {
860   tcp_client_context_t *ctx = (tcp_client_context_t *)arg;
861   if (!ctx) {
862     log_error("Client handler: NULL context");
863     return NULL;
864   }
865 
866   acds_server_t *server = (acds_server_t *)ctx->user_data;
867   socket_t client_socket = ctx->client_socket;
868 
869   // Get client IP for logging
870   char client_ip[INET6_ADDRSTRLEN] = {0};
871   tcp_client_context_get_ip(ctx, client_ip, sizeof(client_ip));
872 
873   log_info("Client handler started for %s", client_ip);
874 
875   // Register client in TCP server registry with allocated client data
876   acds_client_data_t *client_data = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acds_client_data_t), acds_client_data_t *);
877   if (!client_data) {
878     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to allocate client data");
879     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
880     return NULL;
881   }
882   memset(client_data, 0, sizeof(*client_data));
883   client_data->joined_session = false;
884   client_data->handshake_complete = false;
885 
886   // Initialize crypto handshake context with client IP name
887   char crypto_name[96];
888   SAFE_SNPRINTF(crypto_name, sizeof(crypto_name), "crypto_discovery_client_%s", client_ip);
889   asciichat_error_t handshake_result = crypto_handshake_init(crypto_name, &client_data->handshake_ctx, true);
890   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
891     log_error("Failed to initialize crypto handshake for client %s", client_ip);
892     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
893     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake");
894     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
895     return NULL;
896   }
897 
898   // Set server identity keys for handshake
899   client_data->handshake_ctx.server_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
900   client_data->handshake_ctx.server_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
901   memcpy(client_data->handshake_ctx.server_public_key.key, server->identity_public, 32);
902   memcpy(client_data->handshake_ctx.server_private_key.key.ed25519, server->identity_secret, 64);
903 
904   if (tcp_server_add_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket, client_data) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
905     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
906     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to register client in registry");
907     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
908     return NULL;
909   }
910 
911   log_debug("Client %s registered (socket=%d, total=%zu)", client_ip, client_socket,
912             tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
913 
914   // Perform crypto handshake (three-step process)
915   log_debug("Performing crypto handshake with client %s", client_ip);
916 
917   // Step 0: Send crypto parameters
918   handshake_result = crypto_handshake_server_send_parameters_socket(&client_data->handshake_ctx, client_socket);
919   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
920     log_warn("Crypto parameters send failed for client %s", client_ip);
921     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
922     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
923     return NULL;
924   }
925 
926   // Step 1: Start handshake (send server key, receive client key)
927   handshake_result = crypto_handshake_server_start_socket(&client_data->handshake_ctx, client_socket);
928   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
929     log_warn("Crypto handshake start failed for client %s", client_ip);
930     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
931     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
932     return NULL;
933   }
934 
935   // Step 2: Authentication challenge (if required)
936   handshake_result = crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge_socket(&client_data->handshake_ctx, client_socket);
937   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
938     log_warn("Crypto handshake auth challenge failed for client %s", client_ip);
939     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
940     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
941     return NULL;
942   }
943 
944   // Step 3: Complete handshake (verify and finalize) - skip if already complete
945   if (client_data->handshake_ctx.state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
946     handshake_result = crypto_handshake_server_complete_socket(&client_data->handshake_ctx, client_socket);
947     if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
948       log_warn("Crypto handshake complete failed for client %s", client_ip);
949       tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
950       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
951       return NULL;
952     }
953   } else {
954     log_debug("Crypto handshake already complete from auth_challenge step for client %s", client_ip);
955   }
956 
957   client_data->handshake_complete = true;
958   log_info("Crypto handshake complete for client %s", client_ip);
959 
960   // Create persistent TCP transport for this client
961   acip_transport_t *transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("acds_tcp_client", client_socket, NULL);
962   if (!transport) {
963     log_error("Failed to create TCP transport for client %s", client_ip);
964     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
965     socket_close(client_socket);
966     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
967     return NULL;
968   }
969 
970   // Store transport and registry ID in client data, and link back from transport
971   client_data->transport = transport;
972   client_data->registry_id = client_socket;
973   transport->user_data = client_data;
974 
975   // Main packet processing loop
976   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->tcp_server.running)) {
977     packet_type_t packet_type;
978     void *payload = NULL;
979     size_t payload_size = 0;
980 
981     // Receive packet (blocking with system timeout)
982     int result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_size);
983     if (result < 0) {
984       // Check error context to distinguish timeout from actual disconnect
985       asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
986       bool has_context = HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx);
987 
988       // Check if this is a timeout (non-fatal) or actual disconnect (fatal)
989       asciichat_error_t error = GET_ERRNO();
990       if (error == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT ||
991           (error == ERROR_NETWORK && has_context && strstr(err_ctx.context_message, "timed out") != NULL)) {
992         // Timeout waiting for next packet - this is normal, continue waiting
993         log_debug("Client %s: receive timeout, continuing to wait for packets", client_ip);
994         if (payload) {
995           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
996         }
997         continue;
998       }
999 
1000       // Actual disconnect or fatal error
1001       log_info("Client %s disconnected", client_ip);
1002       if (payload) {
1003         buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1004       }
1005       break;
1006     }
1007 
1008     log_debug("Received packet type 0x%02X from %s, length=%zu", packet_type, client_ip, payload_size);
1009 
1010     // Multi-key session creation protocol: block non-PING/PONG/SESSION_CREATE messages
1011     if (client_data->in_multikey_session_create) {
1012       bool allowed =
1013           (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PING || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PONG);
1014 
1015       if (!allowed) {
1016         log_warn("Client %s sent packet type 0x%02X during multi-key session creation - only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG "
1017                  "allowed",
1018                  client_ip, packet_type);
1019 
1020         // Send error response using persistent transport
1021         acip_send_error(transport, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
1022                         "Only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG allowed during multi-key session creation");
1023 
1024         // Free payload and continue
1025         if (payload) {
1026           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1027         }
1028         continue;
1029       }
1030     }
1031 
1032     // O(1) ACIP array-based dispatch
1033     // Set server context for callbacks
1034     acip_acds_callbacks_t callbacks = g_acds_callbacks;
1035     callbacks.app_ctx = server;
1036 
1037     asciichat_error_t dispatch_result =
1038         acip_handle_acds_packet(transport, packet_type, payload, payload_size, client_ip, &callbacks);
1039 
1040     if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1041       log_warn("ACIP handler failed for packet type 0x%02X from %s: %s", packet_type, client_ip,
1042                asciichat_error_string(dispatch_result));
1043     }
1044 
1045     // Free payload (allocated by receive_packet via buffer_pool_alloc)
1046     if (payload) {
1047       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1048     }
1049   }
1050 
1051   // Cleanup
1052   tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1053   log_debug("Client %s unregistered (total=%zu)", client_ip, tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
1054 
1055   // Destroy transport before closing socket
1056   acip_transport_destroy(transport);
1057 
1058   socket_close(client_socket);
1059   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1060 
1061   log_info("Client handler finished for %s", client_ip);
1062   return NULL;
1063 }




References acip_handle_acds_packet(),
    acip_send_error(), acip_tcp_transport_create(),
    acip_transport_destroy(), acip_acds_callbacks_t::app_ctx,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_bool(), buffer_pool_free(),
    tcp_client_context_t::client_socket,
    asciichat_error_context_t::context_message,
    crypto_handshake_init(), CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY,
    crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge_socket(),
    crypto_handshake_server_complete_socket(),
    crypto_handshake_server_send_parameters_socket(),
    crypto_handshake_server_start_socket(),
    private_key_t::ed25519, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NETWORK, ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, GET_ERRNO,
    acds_client_data_t::handshake_complete,
    acds_client_data_t::handshake_ctx, HAS_ERRNO,
    acds_server_t::identity_public,
    acds_server_t::identity_secret,
    acds_client_data_t::in_multikey_session_create,
    acds_client_data_t::joined_session, private_key_t::key,
    public_key_t::key, KEY_TYPE_ED25519, log_debug,
    log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE, PACKET_TYPE_PING,
    PACKET_TYPE_PONG, receive_packet(),
    acds_client_data_t::registry_id, tcp_server::running,
    SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, SAFE_SNPRINTF,

    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_public_key, socket_close(),
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state,
    tcp_client_context_get_ip(), acds_server_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_add_client(), tcp_server_get_client_count(),
    tcp_server_reject_client(), tcp_server_remove_client(),
    acds_client_data_t::transport, private_key_t::type,
    public_key_t::type, acip_transport::user_data, and
    tcp_client_context_t::user_data.


Referenced by acds_server_init().






asciichat_error_t acds_server_init (acds_server_t *
  server, const acds_config_t * config)


Initialize discovery server. Loads or generates identity keys,
    opens database, creates session registry, and binds TCP socket.




Parameters


server Server structure to initialize


Config Module Configuration from command-line
  parsing


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 191 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


191                                                                                        {
192   if (!server || !config) {
193     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server or config is NULL");
194   }
195 
196   memset(server, 0, sizeof(*server));
197   memcpy(&server->config, config, sizeof(acds_config_t));
198 
199   // Open database (SQLite as single source of truth)
200   asciichat_error_t result = database_init(config->database_path, &server->db);
201   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
202     return result;
203   }
204 
205   // Initialize rate limiter with SQLite backend
206   server->rate_limiter = rate_limiter_create_sqlite(NULL); // NULL = externally managed DB
207   if (!server->rate_limiter) {
208     database_close(server->db);
209     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create rate limiter");
210   }
211 
212   // Set the database handle for the rate limiter
213   rate_limiter_set_sqlite_db(server->rate_limiter, server->db);
214 
215   // Configure TCP server
216   tcp_server_config_t tcp_config = {
217       .port = config->port,
218       .ipv4_address = (config->address[0] != '\0') ? config->address : NULL,
219       .ipv6_address = (config->address6[0] != '\0') ? config->address6 : NULL,
220       .bind_ipv4 = (config->address[0] != '\0') || (config->address[0] == '\0' && config->address6[0] == '\0'),
221       .bind_ipv6 = (config->address6[0] != '\0') || (config->address[0] == '\0' && config->address6[0] == '\0'),
222       .accept_timeout_sec = 1,
223       .client_handler = acds_client_handler,
224       .user_data = server,
225   };
226 
227   // Initialize TCP server
228   result = tcp_server_init(&server->tcp_server, &tcp_config);
229   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
230     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
231     database_close(server->db);
232     return result;
233   }
234 
235   // Initialize background worker thread pool
236   atomic_store_bool(&server->shutdown, false);
237   server->worker_pool = thread_pool_create("acds_workers");
238   if (!server->worker_pool) {
239     log_warn("Failed to create worker thread pool");
240     tcp_server_destroy(&server->tcp_server);
241     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
242     database_close(server->db);
243     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create worker thread pool");
244   }
245 
246   // Spawn cleanup thread in worker pool
247   if (thread_pool_spawn(server->worker_pool, cleanup_thread_func, server, 0, "cleanup") != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
248     log_warn("Failed to spawn cleanup thread (continuing without cleanup)");
249   }
250 
251   log_info("Discovery server initialized successfully");
252   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
253 }

    acds_config_t::address, acds_config_t::address6,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_store_bool(),
    acds_server_t::config, database_close(),
    database_init(), acds_config_t::database_path,
    acds_server_t::db, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY,
    log_info, log_warn, acds_config_t::port,
    acds_server_t::rate_limiter, rate_limiter_create_sqlite(),
    rate_limiter_destroy(), rate_limiter_set_sqlite_db(),
    SET_ERRNO, acds_server_t::shutdown,
    acds_server_t::tcp_server, tcp_server_destroy(),
    tcp_server_init(), thread_pool_create(),
    thread_pool_spawn(), and acds_server_t::worker_pool.


Referenced by acds_main().






asciichat_error_t acds_server_run (acds_server_t *
  server)


Run discovery server main loop. Accepts client connections and
    spawns handler threads. Blocks until shutdown signal received.




Parameters


server Initialized server structure


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 255 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


255                                                          {
256   if (!server) {
257     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server is NULL");
258   }
259 
260   log_info("Discovery server accepting connections on port %d", server->config.port);
261 
262   // Delegate to TCP server abstraction
263   return tcp_server_run(&server->tcp_server);
264 }






References acds_server_t::config,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, log_info, acds_config_t::port,
    SET_ERRNO, acds_server_t::tcp_server, and
    tcp_server_run().


Referenced by acds_main().






void
  acds_server_shutdown (acds_server_t * server)


Shutdown discovery server. Closes listen socket, stops accepting
    connections, waits for handler threads to exit, closes database, and frees
    resources.




Parameters


server Server structure to clean up


Definition at line 266 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


266                                                  {
267   if (!server) {
268     return;
269   }
270 
271   // Signal shutdown to worker threads
272   atomic_store_bool(&server->shutdown, true);
273 
274   // Shutdown TCP server (closes listen sockets, stops accept loop)
275   tcp_server_destroy(&server->tcp_server);
276 
277   // Wait for all client handler threads to exit
278   size_t remaining_clients;
279   int shutdown_attempts = 0;
280   const int max_shutdown_attempts = 100; // 10 seconds (100 * 100ms)
281 
282   while ((remaining_clients = tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server)) > 0 &&
283          shutdown_attempts < max_shutdown_attempts) {
284     log_debug("Waiting for %zu client handler threads to exit (attempt %d/%d)", remaining_clients,
285               shutdown_attempts + 1, max_shutdown_attempts);
286     platform_sleep_ms(100);
287     shutdown_attempts++;
288   }
289 
290   if (remaining_clients > 0) {
291     log_warn("Server shutdown: %zu client handler threads still running after 10 seconds", remaining_clients);
292   } else if (shutdown_attempts > 0) {
293     log_debug("All client handler threads exited gracefully");
294   }
295 
296   // Stop and destroy worker thread pool (cleanup thread, etc.)
297   if (server->worker_pool) {
298     thread_pool_destroy(server->worker_pool);
299     server->worker_pool = NULL;
300     log_debug("Worker thread pool stopped");
301   }
302 
303   // Destroy rate limiter
304   if (server->rate_limiter) {
305     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
306     server->rate_limiter = NULL;
307   }
308 
309   // Close database
310   if (server->db) {
311     database_close(server->db);
312     server->db = NULL;
313   }
314 
315   log_info("Server shutdown complete");
316 }






References atomic_store_bool(), database_close(),
    acds_server_t::db, log_debug, log_info,
    log_warn, platform_sleep_ms(),
    acds_server_t::rate_limiter, rate_limiter_destroy(),
    acds_server_t::shutdown, acds_server_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_destroy(), tcp_server_get_client_count(),
    thread_pool_destroy(), and acds_server_t::worker_pool.


Referenced by acds_main().






void
  * acds_websocket_client_handler (void * arg)


WebSocket client handler (thread entry point). Processes ACIP
    packets from a WebSocket client connection. Similar to TCP handler but uses
    transport-based APIs for crypto handshake.




Parameters


arg Pointer to
  websocket_client_context_t


Returns


NULL (thread exit value)


Definition at line 1069 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


1069                                                {
1070   websocket_client_context_t *ctx = (websocket_client_context_t *)arg;
1071   if (!ctx) {
1072     log_error("WebSocket client handler: NULL context");
1073     return NULL;
1074   }
1075 
1076   acds_server_t *server = (acds_server_t *)ctx->user_data;
1077   acip_transport_t *transport = ctx->transport;
1078   char *client_ip = ctx->client_ip;
1079 
1080   log_info("WebSocket client handler started for %s", client_ip);
1081 
1082   // Allocate client_data
1083   acds_client_data_t *client_data = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(acds_client_data_t), acds_client_data_t *);
1084   if (!client_data) {
1085     log_error("Failed to allocate client data for WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1086     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1087     return NULL;
1088   }
1089 
1090   client_data->transport = transport;
1091   client_data->joined_session = false;
1092   client_data->handshake_complete = false;
1093   transport->user_data = client_data;
1094 
1095   // Generate synthetic socket ID for tcp_server registry
1096   static _Atomic int g_ws_synthetic_id = INT_MAX;
1097   int synthetic_id = atomic_fetch_sub(&g_ws_synthetic_id, 1);
1098   client_data->registry_id = synthetic_id;
1099 
1100   // Register in tcp_server with synthetic ID (so callbacks can find client_data)
1101   if (tcp_server_add_client(&server->tcp_server, synthetic_id, client_data) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1102     log_error("Failed to register WebSocket client %s in registry", client_ip);
1103     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
1104     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1105     return NULL;
1106   }
1107 
1108   log_debug("WebSocket client %s registered (synthetic_id=%d, total=%zu)", client_ip, synthetic_id,
1109             tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
1110 
1111   // For WebSocket with TLS (wss://), skip crypto handshake since TLS already provides encryption
1112   // For plain ws://, we should still do crypto handshake, but for now we skip it
1113   // TODO: Add crypto handshake support for plain ws:// connections
1114   client_data->handshake_complete = true;
1115   log_info("WebSocket connection from %s - skipping crypto handshake (TLS encryption assumed)", client_ip);
1116 
1117   // Main packet processing loop using transport recv
1118   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->tcp_server.running)) {
1119     void *recv_buffer = NULL;
1120     size_t recv_len = 0;
1121     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1122 
1123     asciichat_error_t recv_result = acip_transport_recv(transport, &recv_buffer, &recv_len, &alloc_buffer);
1124     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1125       // Check if this is a timeout or disconnect
1126       if (recv_result == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1127         log_debug("WebSocket client %s: receive timeout, continuing to wait for packets", client_ip);
1128         if (alloc_buffer) {
1129           SAFE_FREE(alloc_buffer);
1130         }
1131         continue;
1132       }
1133 
1134       // Actual disconnect or fatal error
1135       log_info("WebSocket client %s disconnected", client_ip);
1136       if (alloc_buffer) {
1137         SAFE_FREE(alloc_buffer);
1138       }
1139       break;
1140     }
1141 
1142     // Parse packet header from received data
1143     if (recv_len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
1144       log_warn("WebSocket client %s: received packet too small (%zu bytes)", client_ip, recv_len);
1145       if (alloc_buffer) {
1146         SAFE_FREE(alloc_buffer);
1147       }
1148       continue;
1149     }
1150 
1151     const packet_header_t *header = (const packet_header_t *)recv_buffer;
1152     packet_type_t packet_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(header->type);
1153     size_t payload_size = NET_TO_HOST_U32(header->length);
1154     const void *payload = (const uint8_t *)recv_buffer + sizeof(packet_header_t);
1155 
1156     // Validate payload size
1157     if (recv_len < sizeof(packet_header_t) + payload_size) {
1158       log_warn("WebSocket client %s: packet size mismatch (expected %zu, got %zu)", client_ip,
1159                sizeof(packet_header_t) + payload_size, recv_len);
1160       if (alloc_buffer) {
1161         SAFE_FREE(alloc_buffer);
1162       }
1163       continue;
1164     }
1165 
1166     log_debug("Received packet type 0x%02X from WebSocket client %s, length=%zu", packet_type, client_ip, payload_size);
1167 
1168     // Multi-key session creation protocol: block non-PING/PONG/SESSION_CREATE messages
1169     if (client_data->in_multikey_session_create) {
1170       bool allowed =
1171           (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PING || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PONG);
1172 
1173       if (!allowed) {
1174         log_warn("WebSocket client %s sent packet type 0x%02X during multi-key session creation - only "
1175                  "SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG allowed",
1176                  client_ip, packet_type);
1177         acip_send_error(transport, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
1178                         "Only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG allowed during multi-key session creation");
1179         if (alloc_buffer) {
1180           SAFE_FREE(alloc_buffer);
1181         }
1182         continue;
1183       }
1184     }
1185 
1186     // O(1) ACIP array-based dispatch
1187     acip_acds_callbacks_t callbacks = g_acds_callbacks;
1188     callbacks.app_ctx = server;
1189 
1190     asciichat_error_t dispatch_result =
1191         acip_handle_acds_packet(transport, packet_type, payload, payload_size, client_ip, &callbacks);
1192 
1193     if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1194       log_warn("ACIP handler failed for packet type 0x%02X from WebSocket client %s: %s", packet_type, client_ip,
1195                asciichat_error_string(dispatch_result));
1196     }
1197 
1198     if (alloc_buffer) {
1199       SAFE_FREE(alloc_buffer);
1200     }
1201   }
1202 
1203   // Cleanup
1204   tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, synthetic_id);
1205   log_debug("WebSocket client %s unregistered (total=%zu)", client_ip, tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
1206 
1207   // Transport is owned by WebSocket server, don't destroy it here
1208 
1209   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1210 
1211   log_info("WebSocket client handler finished for %s", client_ip);
1212   return NULL;
1213 }






References acip_handle_acds_packet(),
    acip_send_error(), acip_acds_callbacks_t::app_ctx,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_bool(),
    websocket_client_context_t::client_ip, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, acds_client_data_t::handshake_complete,
    acds_client_data_t::in_multikey_session_create,
    acds_client_data_t::joined_session, packet_header_t::length,
    log_debug, log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    NET_TO_HOST_U16, NET_TO_HOST_U32,
    PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE, PACKET_TYPE_PING,
    PACKET_TYPE_PONG, acds_client_data_t::registry_id,
    tcp_server::running, SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE,
    acds_server_t::tcp_server, tcp_server_add_client(),
    tcp_server_get_client_count(), tcp_server_remove_client(),
    acds_client_data_t::transport,
    websocket_client_context_t::transport, packet_header_t::type,
    acip_transport::user_data, and
    websocket_client_context_t::user_data.


Referenced by acds_main().
